demographics.living_situation: lives with and provides day-to-day support for an elder family member

language_and_literacy.primary_language: Not specified in source (bilingual; the patient's family primarily speaks a language other than English and the patient acts as their interpreter)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My head is splitting open, I feel dizzy, and I literally cannot process another English word today.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The headache and dizziness came on this afternoon and haven't let up; the patient says they cannot safely keep going and an elder family member with them made contact for help.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on gradually over about six hours this afternoon, building while the patient spent a long hospital appointment translating complex information back and forth for an elder family member
symptom_narrative.character: a tight, band-like pressure squeezing both sides of the head, across the temples and into the jaw and the back of the neck
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: says it is severe and that their head 'is splitting open'
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both sides of the head, the temples, the jaw and the top of the neck
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, not really coming in waves
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; a dark quiet room helps a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: bright light, and being asked to keep reading or translating
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feels dizzy and light-headed, as if they might faint if they stand; feels mentally foggy and keeps losing words in both languages; bright light is uncomfortable
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: says they often get this kind of head pressure after long periods of translating and concentrating, but not usually this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot safely keep going with the day or with helping the family member; had to stop and sit down

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: paracetamol (took 1000 mg a couple of hours ago)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: The patient is the main person who translates medical and official information for an elder family member and had just spent about six hours interpreting at a complex hospital appointment. They describe feeling exhausted and frustrated, worried about getting a translation wrong and causing harm, and upset at being spoken to slowly and loudly by staff. A large stack of untranslated hospital discharge paperwork is waiting at home.

objective_data_offered.value: elder family member says the patient keeps holding and rubbing both temples and has turned the lights off
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: reported by an accompanying family member, not measured

objective_data_offered.value: elder family member says the patient has been unable to concentrate and keeps losing their words in both languages since the appointment ended
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: family-reported impression, not a formal cognitive assessment

objective_data_offered.value: patient says they feel dizzy and as if they might faint when they stand up
